---
title: "Survival analysis based on CNV"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Cmd+Shift+Enter*. 

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(survival)
# install.packages("survminer")       # installs survminer + its deps (ggplot2, ggpubr, etc.)
library(survminer)
#install.packages("xfun")   # upgrades to the current CRAN version (â‰Ą 0.52)
```

```{r}
##  FILE PATHS  
setwd("/Users/annabelshinichen/Desktop/SchoolWork/MSBMI/Davoli_lab/bulk seq/MSK_survival")   # change if needed

# Load clinical data
clin_pt  <- read.delim("data_clinical_patient.txt", skip = 4, check.names = FALSE)
clin_smp <- read.delim("data_clinical_sample.txt", skip = 4, check.names = FALSE)

# Load CNV table and clean SAMPLE_ID
cnv_raw <- read.delim("all_cnv_table.txt", check.names = FALSE, quote = "\"") %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("SAMPLE_ID")

# Identify arm-level CNV columns
arm_cols <- grep("^Arm", names(cnv_raw), value = TRUE)

# Categorize CNVs into Gain / Loss / None
cnv_cat <- cnv_raw %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(arm_cols), ~ case_when(
    .x >  0.2 ~ "Gain",
    .x < -0.2 ~ "Loss",
    TRUE      ~ "None"
  )))
```

# (optional) Look at data 
```{r}
# Convert SAMPLE_ID columns to character to avoid join issues
clin_smp$SAMPLE_ID <- as.character(clin_smp$SAMPLE_ID)
cnv_raw$SAMPLE_ID  <- as.character(cnv_raw$SAMPLE_ID)
```

```{r}
#READ TABLES
clin_pt  <- read.delim(f_patient, skip = 4, check.names = FALSE)
head(clin_pt)
unique(clin_pt$DRUG_TYPE) # 3 types: "PD-1/PDL-1" "Combo"      "CTLA4"     
```



```{r}
cnv_raw <- read.delim("all_cnv_table.txt", check.names = FALSE)

## move rowâ€‘names (sample IDs) into a new column called SAMPLE_ID
cnv_raw <- cnv_raw %>% 
             rownames_to_column(var = "SAMPLE_ID")

head(cnv_raw)
```

```{r}
head(clin_smp)
```


## merge 
```{r}
# Step 1: Prepare clean sample info
sample_info <- clin_smp %>%
  select(SAMPLE_ID, PATIENT_ID, CANCER_TYPE, ONCOTREE_CODE, SAMPLE_TYPE)

# Step 2: Join sample_info with CNV matrix
merged_sample <- sample_info %>%
  left_join(cnv_cat, by = "SAMPLE_ID")

# Step 3: Join patient-level clinical data
df <- clin_pt %>%
  select(PATIENT_ID, OS_MONTHS, OS_STATUS, SEX, DRUG_TYPE) %>%
  left_join(merged_sample, by = "PATIENT_ID") %>%
  mutate(OS = if_else(str_detect(OS_STATUS, "LIVING"), 0, 1))
```

## run cox models 
```{r}
results <- data.frame()

for (arm in grep("^Arm", names(df), value = TRUE)) {
  for (comparison in c("Gain", "Loss")) {
    subset <- df[df[[arm]] %in% c("None", comparison),]
    
    # Skip if fewer than 10 samples or not enough variation
    if (nrow(subset) >= 10 && length(unique(subset[[arm]])) == 2) {
      subset[[arm]] <- factor(subset[[arm]], levels = c(comparison, "None"))
      fit <- coxph(Surv(OS_MONTHS, OS) ~ subset[[arm]], data = subset)
      hr <- exp(coef(fit))
      ci <- round(exp(confint(fit)), 3)
      p <- summary(fit)$coefficients[5]
      
      # Append one row per CANCER_TYPE group within the subset
      results <- rbind(results, subset %>%
        group_by(CANCER_TYPE) %>%
        summarize(
          Arm = arm,
          Comparison = paste(comparison, "vs None"),
          HR = round(hr, 3),
          CI = paste(ci[1], ci[2], sep = " - "),
          P = signif(p, 3),
          log2HR = log2(hr),
          Event = comparison,
          N = n()
        )
      )
    }
  }
}

# save output
# write.csv(results, "CNV_HR_table.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```


Comparing p values 
- Rregression model 
- Test HR = relative risk over time 
  - Can adjust for co-variates 
- HR = 2 --> 2x higher risk 
```{r}
# Cox significant hits p < 0.01 
cox_sig_hits <- results %>% filter(P < 0.1)
nrow(cox_sig_hits)
```



another try of filtering by omly PD1 on cancer type 
```{r}
results <- data.frame()

for (tumor in unique(df$CANCER_TYPE)) {
  tumor_data <- df %>%
    filter(CANCER_TYPE == tumor,
           DRUG_TYPE == "PD-1/PDL-1")  # Only PD-1/PDL-1 treated patients

  for (arm in grep("^Arm", names(tumor_data), value = TRUE)) {
    for (comparison in c("Gain", "Loss")) {

      subset <- tumor_data %>%
        filter(!!sym(arm) %in% c("None", comparison)) %>%
        mutate(CNV = factor(!!sym(arm), levels = c(comparison, "None")))

      # Only run if enough samples and both CNV groups are present
      if (nrow(subset) >= 10 && length(unique(subset$CNV)) == 2) {
        fit <- coxph(Surv(OS_MONTHS, OS) ~ CNV, data = subset)
        hr <- exp(coef(fit))
        ci <- round(exp(confint(fit)), 3)
        p <- summary(fit)$coefficients[5]

        results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
          CANCER_TYPE = tumor,
          Arm = arm,
          Comparison = paste(comparison, "vs None"),
          HR = round(hr, 3),
          CI = paste(ci[1], ci[2], sep = " - "),
          P = signif(p, 3),
          log2HR = log2(hr),
          Event = comparison,
          N = nrow(subset)
        ))
      }
    }
  }
}
```



```{r}
# KM plot example: Arm9p Loss vs None in Melanoma
km_data <- df %>%
  filter(CANCER_TYPE == "Melanoma", Arm9p %in% c("Loss", "None")) %>%
  mutate(CNVstatus = factor(Arm9p, levels = c("Loss", "None")))

km_fit <- survfit(Surv(OS_MONTHS, OS) ~ CNVstatus, data = km_data)

ggsurvplot(km_fit, data = km_data,
           pval = TRUE, risk.table = TRUE,
           palette = c("#D64045", "grey20"),
           title = "Melanoma â€“ Arm9p Loss vs None",
           xlab = "Time (months)", ylab = "Overall survival")
```


## plot arms with p<0.01 based on MSK survival 
```{r}
# Manually define significant tumor-arm-comparison combinations
sig_hits <- tribble(
  ~CANCER_TYPE, ~Arm,     ~Comparison,
  "Bladder Cancer", "Arm11p", "Loss",
  "Colorectal Cancer", "Arm20q", "Gain",
  "Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer", "Arm8q", "Loss",
  "Melanoma", "Arm1q", "Gain",
  "Renal Cell Carcinoma", "Arm22q", "Loss",
  "Melanoma", "Arm3q", "Loss",
  "Bladder Cancer", "Arm21q", "Loss",
  "Bladder Cancer", "Arm9q", "Loss",
  "Head and Neck Cancer", "Arm14q", "Loss",
  "Colorectal Cancer", "Arm13q", "Gain",
  "Renal Cell Carcinoma", "Arm17p", "Gain"
)


# Output folder
output_dir <- "KM_plots" # already in the current working directory 

# Loop over each hit and generate KM plots by drug type
for (i in 1:nrow(sig_hits)) {
  tumor_type <- sig_hits$CANCER_TYPE[i]
  arm <- sig_hits$Arm[i]
  comparison <- sig_hits$Comparison[i]

  for (drug in c("PD-1/PDL-1", "CTLA4", "Combo")) {
    subset <- df %>%
      filter(CANCER_TYPE == tumor_type,
             DRUG_TYPE == drug,
             !!sym(arm) %in% c("None", comparison)) %>%
      mutate(CNVstatus = factor(!!sym(arm), levels = c(comparison, "None")))

    if (nrow(subset) >= 10 && length(unique(subset$CNVstatus)) == 2) {
      surv_obj <- Surv(subset$OS_MONTHS, subset$OS)
      fit <- survfit(surv_obj ~ CNVstatus, data = subset)

      p <- ggsurvplot(fit, data = subset,
                      pval = TRUE, risk.table = TRUE,
                      palette = c("#D64045", "grey20"),
                      title = paste(tumor_type, arm, comparison, "vs None â€“", drug),
                      xlab = "Time (months)", ylab = "Overall survival")

      filename <- paste0(output_dir, "/", gsub(" ", "_", tumor_type), "_", arm, "_", comparison, "_", gsub("/", "", drug), ".pdf")
      ggsave(filename, plot = p$plot, width = 7, height = 5)
    }
  }
}
```


### by oncotree code 
```{r}
unique(df$ONCOTREE_CODE)
```

```{r}
# Redefine significant hits using ONCOTREE_CODE instead of CANCER_TYPE
sig_hits <- tribble(
  ~ONCOTREE_CODE, ~Arm,   ~Comparison,
  "LUAD",         "Arm1q",  "Gain",
  "LUAD",         "Arm5q",  "Loss",
  "LUAD",         "Arm6q",  "Loss",
  "LUAD",         "Arm9p",  "Loss",
  "SKCM",         "Arm6q",  "Loss",
  "SKCM",         "Arm9p",  "Loss",
  "PAAD",         "Arm1q",  "Gain",
  "PAAD",         "Arm6q",  "Loss",
  "PAAD",         "Arm9p",  "Loss",
  "HNSC",  "Arm5q",  "Loss",
  "HNSC",  "Arm9p",  "Loss",
  "UCEC",     "Arm1q",  "Gain",
  "BLCA",         "Arm9p",  "Loss",
  "BLCA",         "Arm11p", "Loss"
)

# Output folder
output_dir <- "KM_plots_by_oncotree"
dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Loop over each hit and generate KM plots by drug type
for (i in 1:nrow(sig_hits)) {
  onco_code <- sig_hits$ONCOTREE_CODE[i]
  arm <- sig_hits$Arm[i]
  comparison <- sig_hits$Comparison[i]

  for (drug in c("PD-1/PDL-1", "CTLA4", "Combo")) {
    subset <- df %>%
      filter(ONCOTREE_CODE == onco_code,
             DRUG_TYPE == drug,
             !!sym(arm) %in% c("None", comparison)) %>%
      mutate(CNVstatus = factor(!!sym(arm), levels = c(comparison, "None")))

    if (nrow(subset) >= 10 && length(unique(subset$CNVstatus)) == 2) {
      surv_obj <- Surv(subset$OS_MONTHS, subset$OS)
      fit <- survfit(surv_obj ~ CNVstatus, data = subset)

      p <- ggsurvplot(fit, data = subset,
                      pval = TRUE, risk.table = TRUE,
                      palette = c("#D64045", "grey20"),
                      title = paste(onco_code, arm, comparison, "vs None â€“", drug),
                      xlab = "Time (months)", ylab = "Overall survival")

      filename <- paste0(output_dir, "/", onco_code, "_", arm, "_", comparison, "_", gsub("/", "", drug), ".pdf")
      ggsave(filename, plot = p$plot, width = 7, height = 5)
    }
  }
}
```

## PD1 only 
count p < 0.01
```{r}
km_sig_hits <- data.frame()

for (i in 1:nrow(cox_sig_hits)) {
  tumor_type <- cox_sig_hits$CANCER_TYPE[i]
  arm <- cox_sig_hits$Arm[i]
  comparison <- cox_sig_hits$Event[i]

  subset <- df %>%
    filter(CANCER_TYPE == tumor_type,
           DRUG_TYPE == "PD-1/PDL-1",
           !!sym(arm) %in% c("None", comparison)) %>%
    mutate(CNVstatus = factor(!!sym(arm), levels = c(comparison, "None")))

  if (length(unique(subset$CNVstatus)) == 2) {
    surv_obj <- Surv(subset$OS_MONTHS, subset$OS)
    surv_diff <- survdiff(surv_obj ~ CNVstatus, data = subset)
    km_p <- 1 - pchisq(surv_diff$chisq, df = 1)

    if (km_p < 0.5) {
      km_sig_hits <- rbind(km_sig_hits, data.frame(
        CANCER_TYPE = tumor_type,
        Arm = arm,
        Comparison = comparison,
        CoxP = cox_sig_hits$P[i],
        KMP = signif(km_p, 3)
      ))
    }
  }
}

nrow(km_sig_hits)
```


Dual p-value filtering
```{r}
# Output directory
output_dir <- "KM_plots_PD1"
# dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Initialize table of filtered hits
km_sig_hits <- data.frame()

# Loop through Cox-significant hits
for (i in 1:nrow(cox_sig_hits)) {
  tumor_type <- cox_sig_hits$CANCER_TYPE[i]
  arm <- cox_sig_hits$Arm[i]
  comparison <- cox_sig_hits$Event[i]
  cox_p <- cox_sig_hits$P[i]

  subset <- df %>%
    filter(CANCER_TYPE == tumor_type,
           DRUG_TYPE == "PD-1/PDL-1",
           !!sym(arm) %in% c("None", comparison)) %>%
    mutate(CNVstatus = factor(!!sym(arm), levels = c(comparison, "None")))

  if (nrow(subset) >= 10) {
    surv_obj <- Surv(subset$OS_MONTHS, subset$OS)
    surv_diff <- survdiff(surv_obj ~ CNVstatus, data = subset)
    km_p <- 1 - pchisq(surv_diff$chisq, df = 1)

    if (km_p < 0.1) {
      # Save row to table
      km_sig_hits <- rbind(km_sig_hits, data.frame(
        CANCER_TYPE = tumor_type,
        Arm = arm,
        Comparison = comparison,
        CoxP = signif(cox_p, 3),
        KMP = signif(km_p, 3)
      ))

      # KM plot
      fit <- survfit(surv_obj ~ CNVstatus, data = subset)
      p <- ggsurvplot(fit, data = subset,
                      pval = TRUE,
                      risk.table = TRUE,
                      palette = c("#D64045", "grey20"),
                      title = paste(tumor_type, arm, comparison, "vs None â€“ PD-1/PDL-1"),
                      xlab = "Survival Time (months)",
                      ylab = "Survival probability",
                      risk.table.height = 0.25,
                      break.time.by = 20,
                      ggtheme = theme_classic(base_size = 14))

      # Save plot
      filename <- paste0(output_dir, "/", gsub(" ", "_", tumor_type), "_", arm, "_", comparison, "_PD1.pdf")
      ggsave(filename, plot = p$plot, width = 7, height = 5)
    }
  }
}

# Optional: View summary of significant dual-p hits
km_sig_hits
```


# survival analysis for all arms
```{r}
library(survival)
library(dplyr)

# Define arm-level columns
arm_cols <- grep("^Arm", names(df_cyto), value = TRUE)

# Initialize output data frames
results_arm_cancer_type <- data.frame()
results_arm_oncotree <- data.frame()

### 3a. Loop by CANCER_TYPE
for (tumor in unique(df_cyto$CANCER_TYPE)) {
  tumor_data <- df_cyto %>% filter(CANCER_TYPE == tumor, DRUG_TYPE == "PD-1/PDL-1")

  for (arm in arm_cols) {
    for (comparison in c("Gain", "Loss")) {
      subset <- tumor_data %>%
        filter(!!sym(arm) %in% c("None", comparison)) %>%
        mutate(CNV = factor(!!sym(arm), levels = c("None", comparison))) %>%
        filter(!is.na(OS), !is.na(OS_MONTHS))

      if (nrow(subset) >= 10 && length(unique(subset$CNV)) == 2) {
        surv_obj <- Surv(subset$OS_MONTHS, subset$OS)

        # Try Cox
        fit <- tryCatch(coxph(surv_obj ~ CNV, data = subset), error = function(e) NULL)
        if (is.null(fit)) next

        cnv_term <- grep("^CNV", names(coef(fit)), value = TRUE)
        if (length(cnv_term) != 1) next

        hr <- exp(coef(fit)[cnv_term])
        ci <- round(exp(confint(fit)[cnv_term, ]), 3)
        cox_p <- summary(fit)$coefficients[cnv_term, "Pr(>|z|)"]

        # Try log-rank
        logrank_p <- tryCatch({
          sdiff <- survdiff(surv_obj ~ CNV, data = subset)
          1 - pchisq(sdiff$chisq, df = 1)
        }, error = function(e) NA)

        results_arm_cancer_type <- rbind(results_arm_cancer_type, data.frame(
          CANCER_TYPE = tumor,
          Arm = arm,
          Comparison = comparison,
          HR = round(hr, 3),
          log2HR = log2(hr),
          CI = paste(ci[1], ci[2], sep = " - "),
          CoxP = signif(cox_p, 3),
          LogRankP = signif(logrank_p, 3),
          N = nrow(subset)
        ))
      }
    }
  }
}

### 3b. Loop by ONCOTREE_CODE
for (code in unique(df_cyto$ONCOTREE_CODE)) {
  tumor_data <- df_cyto %>% filter(ONCOTREE_CODE == code, DRUG_TYPE == "PD-1/PDL-1")

  for (arm in arm_cols) {
    for (comparison in c("Gain", "Loss")) {
      subset <- tumor_data %>%
        filter(!!sym(arm) %in% c("None", comparison)) %>%
        mutate(CNV = factor(!!sym(arm), levels = c("None", comparison))) %>%
        filter(!is.na(OS), !is.na(OS_MONTHS))

      if (nrow(subset) >= 10 && length(unique(subset$CNV)) == 2) {
        surv_obj <- Surv(subset$OS_MONTHS, subset$OS)

        # Try Cox
        fit <- tryCatch(coxph(surv_obj ~ CNV, data = subset), error = function(e) NULL)
        if (is.null(fit)) next

        cnv_term <- grep("^CNV", names(coef(fit)), value = TRUE)
        if (length(cnv_term) != 1) next

        hr <- exp(coef(fit)[cnv_term])
        ci <- round(exp(confint(fit)[cnv_term, ]), 3)
        cox_p <- summary(fit)$coefficients[cnv_term, "Pr(>|z|)"]

        # Try log-rank
        logrank_p <- tryCatch({
          sdiff <- survdiff(surv_obj ~ CNV, data = subset)
          1 - pchisq(sdiff$chisq, df = 1)
        }, error = function(e) NA)

        results_arm_oncotree <- rbind(results_arm_oncotree, data.frame(
          ONCOTREE_CODE = code,
          Arm = arm,
          Comparison = comparison,
          HR = round(hr, 3),
          log2HR = log2(hr),
          CI = paste(ci[1], ci[2], sep = " - "),
          CoxP = signif(cox_p, 3),
          LogRankP = signif(logrank_p, 3),
          N = nrow(subset)
        ))
      }
    }
  }
}

# Save both results
write.csv(results_arm_cancer_type, "ArmLevel_Survival_CancerType.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(results_arm_oncotree, "ArmLevel_Survival_OncoTree.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```


Plot by oncotree 
```{r}
# Output directory
output_dir <- "KM_plots_PD1_oncotree"
dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Store results
km_sig_hits_oncotree <- data.frame()

# Get arm columns
arm_cols <- grep("^Arm", names(df), value = TRUE)

# Loop by ONCOTREE_CODE Ă— arm Ă— Gain/Loss
for (oncocode in unique(df$ONCOTREE_CODE)) {
  tumor_data <- df %>% filter(ONCOTREE_CODE == oncocode, DRUG_TYPE == "PD-1/PDL-1")

  for (arm in arm_cols) {
    for (comparison in c("Gain", "Loss")) {

      subset <- tumor_data %>%
        filter(!!sym(arm) %in% c("None", comparison)) %>%
        mutate(CNVstatus = factor(!!sym(arm), levels = c(comparison, "None")))

      if (nrow(subset) >= 10 && length(unique(subset$CNVstatus)) == 2) {

        # Cox model
        fit <- tryCatch(coxph(Surv(OS_MONTHS, OS) ~ CNVstatus, data = subset), error = function(e) NULL)
        if (is.null(fit)) next

        cox_p <- summary(fit)$coefficients["CNVstatusNone", "Pr(>|z|)"]

        # Log-rank
        surv_obj <- Surv(subset$OS_MONTHS, subset$OS)
        km_p <- tryCatch({
          surv_diff <- survdiff(surv_obj ~ CNVstatus, data = subset)
          1 - pchisq(surv_diff$chisq, df = 1)
        }, error = function(e) NA)

        if (!is.na(km_p) && cox_p < 0.05 && km_p < 0.1) {
          km_sig_hits_oncotree <- rbind(km_sig_hits_oncotree, data.frame(
            ONCOTREE_CODE = oncocode,
            Arm = arm,
            Comparison = comparison,
            CoxP = signif(cox_p, 3),
            LogRankP = signif(km_p, 3),
            N = nrow(subset)
          ))

          # KM plot
          surv_fit <- survfit(surv_obj ~ CNVstatus, data = subset)
          p <- ggsurvplot(surv_fit, data = subset,
                          pval = TRUE,
                          risk.table = TRUE,
                          palette = c("#D64045", "grey20"),
                          title = paste(oncocode, arm, comparison, "vs None â€“ PD-1/PDL-1"),
                          xlab = "Survival Time (months)",
                          ylab = "Survival probability",
                          risk.table.height = 0.25,
                          break.time.by = 20,
                          ggtheme = theme_classic(base_size = 14))

          filename <- paste0(output_dir, "/", oncocode, "_", arm, "_", comparison, "_PD1.pdf")
          ggsave(filename, plot = p$plot, width = 7, height = 5)
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

# HR curve (unadjusted)
```{r}
results$logHR <- log2(results$HR)
results$Event <- ifelse(grepl("Gain", results$Comparison), "Gain", "Loss")

ggplot(results %>% filter(P < 0.05), aes(x = Arm, y = logHR, fill = Event)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge()) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Gain" = "firebrick", "Loss" = "steelblue")) +
  coord_flip() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Survival HR by Arm-level CNV",
       y = "log2(Hazard Ratio)",
       x = "Chromosome Arm") +
  theme_bw()
```

** Show which chromosomal arms are associatied with higher or lower survival (via log2(HR)) across multiple tumor types. **
### Arm 
```{r}
# 1. filter for tumor types of interest 
tumors_to_plot <- c("Bladder Cancer", "Colorectal Cancer", "Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer", 
                    "Melanoma", "Renal Cell Carcinoma", "Head and Neck Cancer")
results_filtered <- results %>%
  filter(CANCER_TYPE %in% tumors_to_plot, P < 0.05)

# 2. calculate log2(HR) and add CNV types 
results_filtered <- results_filtered %>%
  mutate(
    log2HR = log2(HR),
    Event = ifelse(str_detect(Comparison, "Gain"), "Gain", "Loss")
  )

# 3. plot chromosomes in order 
results_filtered$Arm <- factor(results_filtered$Arm, 
  levels = unique(results_filtered$Arm)[order(as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9]", "", unique(results_filtered$Arm))))])

# 4. Plot 
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(results_filtered, aes(x = Arm, y = log2HR, fill = Event)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "identity") +
  facet_wrap(~ CANCER_TYPE, scales = "free_x", ncol = 2) +
  coord_flip() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Gain" = "#D64045", "Loss" = "#375E97")) +
  labs(title = "CNV Arm-level Effects on Survival",
       x = "Chromosomal Arm", y = "log2(Hazard Ratio)",
       fill = "CNV Event") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))
```

```{r}
# Make sure CANCER_TYPE is in `results`
results_all <- results %>%
  filter(CANCER_TYPE %in% tumors_to_plot) %>%
  mutate(
    log2HR = log2(HR),
    sign_logP = -log10(P) * ifelse(log2HR > 0, 1, -1),
    Event = ifelse(str_detect(Comparison, "Gain"), "Gain", "Loss")
  )

# Order arms by chromosome number
results_all$Arm <- factor(results_all$Arm,
  levels = unique(results_all$Arm)[order(as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9XY]", "", unique(results_all$Arm))))])

# Plot all arms
ggplot(results_all, aes(x = Arm, y = sign_logP, fill = Event)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "identity") +
  facet_wrap(~ CANCER_TYPE, scales = "free_x", ncol = 2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = c(-1.3, 1.3), linetype = "dotted", color = "grey40") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Gain" = "#D64045", "Loss" = "#375E97")) +
  labs(
    title = "Survival Associations by CNV Arm",
    y = "Signed -log10(p)",         # Or change to "log2(Hazard Ratio)" if you prefer
    x = "Chromosome Arm",
    fill = "CNV Event"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```

```{r}
# Create output folder
dir.create("logHR_plots", showWarnings = FALSE)

# Unique cancer types
cancer_types <- unique(results_all$CANCER_TYPE)

# Loop and plot one by one
for (cancer in cancer_types) {
  p <- results_all %>%
    filter(CANCER_TYPE == cancer) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = Arm, y = sign_logP, fill = Event)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "identity") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = c(-1.3, 1.3), linetype = "dotted", color = "grey40") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Gain" = "#D64045", "Loss" = "#375E97")) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Survival Associations by CNV Arm â€“", cancer),
      y = "Signed -log10(p)",
      x = "Chromosome Arm",
      fill = "CNV Event"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    )

  # Save each as PDF
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("logHR_plots/", gsub(" ", "_", cancer), "_survival_CNV_arm.pdf"),
    plot = p, width = 8, height = 5
  )
}
```

## cytoband level HR 
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

# 1. Identify cytoband-level CNV columns
cytoband_cols <- grep("^[0-9XY]+[pq]", names(cnv_raw), value = TRUE)

# 2. Categorize CNVs at the cytoband level
cnv_cat_cytoband <- cnv_raw %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(cytoband_cols), ~ case_when(
    .x >  0.2 ~ "Gain",
    .x < -0.2 ~ "Loss",
    TRUE      ~ "None"
  )))

# 3. Merge sample-level metadata (including ONCOTREE and CANCER_TYPE)
sample_info <- clin_smp %>%
  select(SAMPLE_ID, PATIENT_ID, CANCER_TYPE, ONCOTREE_CODE)

# 4. Join CNV categories to sample metadata
merged_sample_cytoband <- merge(sample_info, cnv_cat_cytoband, by = "SAMPLE_ID")

# 5. Join survival metadata (OS, OS_MONTHS)
df_cyto <- merge(
  clin_pt[, c("PATIENT_ID", "OS_MONTHS", "OS_STATUS")],
  merged_sample_cytoband,
  by = "PATIENT_ID"
) %>%
  mutate(
    OS = if_else(str_detect(OS_STATUS, "LIVING"), 0, 1)
  )
```

Plot

### By cancer types 
```{r}
# Define cancer types
cancer_types_to_plot <- c(
  "Bladder Cancer",
  "Colorectal Cancer",
  "Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer",
  "Melanoma",
  "Renal Cell Carcinoma",
  "Head and Neck Cancer",
  "Glioma",
  "Esophagogastric Cancer",
  "Breast Cancer"
)

# 3. Filter only tumor types of interest
df_cyto_filtered <- df_cyto %>% filter(CANCER_TYPE %in% cancer_types_to_plot)

# 4. Initialize results data frame
results_cytoband <- data.frame()

for (tumor in cancer_types_to_plot) {
  tumor_data <- df_cyto_filtered %>% filter(CANCER_TYPE == tumor)

  for (band in cytoband_cols) {
    for (comparison in c("Gain", "Loss")) {
      subset <- tumor_data %>%
        filter(!!sym(band) %in% c("None", comparison)) %>%
        mutate(CNV = factor(!!sym(band), levels = c(comparison, "None")))

      if (nrow(subset) >= 10 && length(unique(subset$CNV)) == 2) {
        tryCatch({
          fit <- coxph(Surv(OS_MONTHS, OS) ~ CNV, data = subset)

          # Skip models with infinite coefficients
          beta <- coef(fit)
          if (any(!is.finite(beta))) next

          hr <- exp(beta)
          ci <- round(exp(confint(fit)), 3)
          p <- summary(fit)$coefficients[5]

          results_cytoband <- rbind(results_cytoband, data.frame(
            CANCER_TYPE = tumor,
            Cytoband = band,
            Comparison = comparison,
            HR = round(hr, 3),
            log2HR = log2(hr),
            CI = paste(ci[1], ci[2], sep = " - "),
            P = signif(p, 3),
            N = nrow(subset)
          ))
        }, error = function(e) {
          message(paste("Skipped:", tumor, band, comparison, "-", e$message))
        })
      }
    }
  }
}

```

Plot cytoband level log2(HR)
```{r}

# Add columns for plotting
results_cytoband <- results_cytoband %>%
  mutate(
    Event = ifelse(Comparison == "Gain", "Gain", "Loss"),
    Chrom = str_extract(Cytoband, "^[0-9XY]+"),
    ArmLabel = str_extract(Cytoband, "^[0-9XY]+[pq]")
  )

# Sort cytobands in genomic order
cyto_levels <- results_cytoband %>%
  distinct(Cytoband) %>%
  mutate(order = as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9XY]", "", Cytoband))) %>%
  arrange(order) %>%
  pull(Cytoband)

results_cytoband$Cytoband <- factor(results_cytoband$Cytoband, levels = unique(cyto_levels))

# Create mapping: one cytoband per arm for label
axis_labels <- results_cytoband %>%
  group_by(ArmLabel) %>%
  summarise(Cytoband = Cytoband[ceiling(n()/2)])

# Plot cytoband-level, label by arm
ggplot(results_cytoband, aes(x = Cytoband, y = log2HR, fill = Event)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ CANCER_TYPE, scales = "free_x", ncol = 3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Gain" = "#D64045", "Loss" = "#375E97")) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    breaks = axis_labels$Cytoband,
    labels = axis_labels$ArmLabel
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Cytoband-level CNV Survival Associations (log2 HR)",
    x = "Chromosome Arm (labeled)", y = "log2(Hazard Ratio)", fill = "CNV Event"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, size = 6),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10)
  )
```


Plot individually 
```{r}
# Create output directory if it doesn't exist
# dir.create("logHR_plots_cytoband", showWarnings = FALSE)

# Extract plotting labels (once globally)
axis_labels <- results_cytoband %>%
  group_by(ArmLabel) %>%
  summarise(Cytoband = Cytoband[ceiling(n()/2)], .groups = "drop")

# Correct genomic order sorting
cyto_levels <- results_cytoband %>%
  mutate(
    chr_num = as.numeric(str_extract(Cytoband, "^[0-9]+")),
    chr_num = ifelse(is.na(chr_num), ifelse(str_detect(Cytoband, "^X"), 23, 24), chr_num),
    arm_suffix = str_extract(Cytoband, "[pq]$")
  ) %>%
  arrange(chr_num, arm_suffix) %>%
  pull(Cytoband) %>%
  unique()

results_cytoband$Cytoband <- factor(results_cytoband$Cytoband, levels = unique(cyto_levels))

# Loop over each cancer type and plot individually
for (tumor in unique(results_cytoband$CANCER_TYPE)) {
  plot_data <- results_cytoband %>% filter(CANCER_TYPE == tumor)

  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Cytoband, y = log2HR, fill = Event)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Gain" = "#D64045", "Loss" = "#375E97")) +
    scale_x_discrete(
      breaks = axis_labels$Cytoband,
      labels = axis_labels$ArmLabel
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("CNV Survival Effect in ", tumor),
      x = "Chromosome Arm (labeled)",
      y = "log2(Hazard Ratio)",
      fill = "CNV Event"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, size = 6),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
    )

  # Save individual PDF
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("logHR_plots_cytoband/", gsub(" ", "_", tumor), "_cytoband_logHR.pdf"),
    plot = p, width = 10, height = 4
  )
}
```


	â€˘	log2(HR) > 0 â†’ CNV increases risk of death (hazard â†‘)
	+ 0.5 = 41% higher risk 
	+ 1 = 2x higher risk (bad prognosis)
	â€˘	log2(HR) < 0 â†’ CNV decreases risk of death (hazard â†“)
	â€˘	log2(HR) = 0 â†’ No effect (HR = 1)
	
	
**UPDATE: filter for p value**
```{r}
dir.create("logHR_plots_filtered", showWarnings = FALSE)

for (tumor in unique(results_cytoband$CANCER_TYPE)) {
  plot_data <- results_cytoband %>%
    filter(CANCER_TYPE == tumor, P < 0.05)  # filter for p value 

  if (nrow(plot_data) == 0) next  # skip if no significant results

  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Cytoband, y = log2HR, fill = Event)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Gain" = "#D64045", "Loss" = "#375E97")) +
    scale_x_discrete(
      breaks = axis_labels$Cytoband,
      labels = axis_labels$ArmLabel
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Significant CNV Survival Effects in ", tumor),
      x = "Chromosome",
      y = "log2(Hazard Ratio)",
      fill = "CNV Event"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, size = 6),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
    )

  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("logHR_plots_filtered/", gsub(" ", "_", tumor), ".pdf"),
    plot = p, width = 10, height = 4
  )
}
```

### Plot by oncotree 
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)
library(survival)

# Step 1: Define ONCOTREE_CODEs of interest: based on > 30 counts 
oncotree_codes_to_plot <- c("LUAD", "SKCM", "CCRCC", "BLCA", "GBM", "COAD", "LUSC",
                            "MUP", "UTUC", "IDC", "STAD", "OPHSC", "ESCA", "UM", "HNSC")

# Step 2: Filter data
df_cyto_filtered <- df_cyto %>% filter(ONCOTREE_CODE %in% oncotree_codes_to_plot)

# Step 3: Run Cox regression by ONCOTREE and cytoband
results_cytoband <- data.frame()

for (code in oncotree_codes_to_plot) {
  tumor_data <- df_cyto_filtered %>% filter(ONCOTREE_CODE == code)
  for (band in cytoband_cols) {
    for (comparison in c("Gain", "Loss")) {
      subset <- tumor_data %>%
        filter(!!sym(band) %in% c("None", comparison)) %>%
        mutate(CNV = factor(!!sym(band), levels = c(comparison, "None")))
      if (nrow(subset) >= 10 && length(unique(subset$CNV)) == 2) {
        tryCatch({
          fit <- coxph(Surv(OS_MONTHS, OS) ~ CNV, data = subset)
          beta <- coef(fit)
          if (any(!is.finite(beta))) next
          hr <- exp(beta)
          ci <- round(exp(confint(fit)), 3)
          p <- summary(fit)$coefficients[5]
          results_cytoband <- rbind(results_cytoband, data.frame(
            ONCOTREE_CODE = code,
            Cytoband = band,
            Comparison = comparison,
            HR = round(hr, 3),
            log2HR = log2(hr),
            CI = paste(ci[1], ci[2], sep = " - "),
            P = signif(p, 3),
            N = nrow(subset)
          ))
        }, error = function(e) {
          message(paste("Skipped:", code, band, comparison, "-", e$message))
        })
      }
    }
  }
}

# Step 4: Add plotting columns
results_cytoband <- results_cytoband %>%
  mutate(
    Event = ifelse(Comparison == "Gain", "Gain", "Loss"),
    Chrom = str_extract(Cytoband, "^[0-9XY]+"),
    ArmLabel = str_extract(Cytoband, "^[0-9XY]+[pq]")
  )

# Step 5: Genomic ordering
cyto_levels <- results_cytoband %>%
  mutate(
    chr_num = as.numeric(str_extract(Cytoband, "^[0-9]+")),
    chr_num = ifelse(is.na(chr_num), ifelse(str_detect(Cytoband, "^X"), 23, 24), chr_num),
    arm_suffix = str_extract(Cytoband, "[pq]$")
  ) %>%
  arrange(chr_num, arm_suffix) %>%
  pull(Cytoband) %>%
  unique()
results_cytoband$Cytoband <- factor(results_cytoband$Cytoband, levels = cyto_levels)

# Step 6: Create label mapping (1 cytoband per arm)
axis_labels <- results_cytoband %>%
  group_by(ArmLabel) %>%
  summarise(Cytoband = Cytoband[ceiling(n() / 2)], .groups = "drop")

# Step 7: Make folder and plot individually
dir.create("logHR_plots_cytoband_oncotree", showWarnings = FALSE)

for (tumor in unique(results_cytoband$ONCOTREE_CODE)) {
  plot_data <- results_cytoband %>% filter(ONCOTREE_CODE == tumor)

  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Cytoband, y = log2HR, fill = Event)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Gain" = "#D64045", "Loss" = "#375E97")) +
    scale_x_discrete(
      breaks = axis_labels$Cytoband,
      labels = axis_labels$ArmLabel
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("CNV Survival Effect in ", tumor),
      x = "Chromosome Arm (labeled)",
      y = "log2(Hazard Ratio)",
      fill = "CNV Event"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, size = 6),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
    )

  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("logHR_plots_cytoband_oncotree/", tumor, "_cytoband_logHR.pdf"),
    plot = p, width = 10, height = 4
  )
}

```


# HR curve with co-variates: purity & aneuploidy 

## assess whether the covariate has impact
```{r}
# Example for Arm1q Gain vs None
subset <- df %>%
  filter(CANCER_TYPE == "Melanoma",
         DRUG_TYPE == "PD-1/PDL-1",
         Arm1q %in% c("Gain", "None")) %>%
  mutate(CNV = factor(Arm1q, levels = c("Gain", "None"))) %>%
  left_join(aneuploidy_cytoband, by = "SAMPLE_ID")

# Test if aneuploidy differs by CNV status
wilcox.test(AneuploidyScore ~ CNV, data = subset)

# Same for purity
wilcox.test(clin_smp$TUMOR_PURITY[match(subset$SAMPLE_ID, clin_smp$SAMPLE_ID)] ~ subset$CNV)

```

Cytoband aneuploidy 

```{r}
# Calculate aneuploidy score for cytoband 
cytoband_cols <- grep("^[0-9XY]+[pq]", names(cnv_raw), value = TRUE)

aneuploidy_cytoband <- cnv_raw %>%
  select(SAMPLE_ID, all_of(cytoband_cols)) %>%
  mutate(AneuploidyScore = rowSums(abs(across(all_of(cytoband_cols))), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  select(SAMPLE_ID, AneuploidyScore)


# merge into df
df$SAMPLE_ID <- as.character(df$SAMPLE_ID)
aneuploidy_cytoband$SAMPLE_ID <- as.character(aneuploidy_cytoband$SAMPLE_ID)

df <- df %>%
  left_join(aneuploidy_cytoband, by = "SAMPLE_ID")

# confirm if column is present
colnames(df)
```

```{r}
library(survival)
library(dplyr)

arm_cols <- grep("^Arm", names(df), value = TRUE)
confounded_tumors <- c()

for (tumor in unique(df$CANCER_TYPE)) {
  for (arm in arm_cols) {
    for (comparison in c("Gain", "Loss")) {

      subset <- df %>%
        filter(CANCER_TYPE == tumor,
               DRUG_TYPE == "PD-1/PDL-1",
               !!sym(arm) %in% c("None", comparison),
               !is.na(AneuploidyScore),
               !is.na(OS),
               !is.na(OS_MONTHS)) %>%
        mutate(CNV = factor(!!sym(arm), levels = c(comparison, "None")))

      if (nrow(subset) >= 10 && length(unique(subset$CNV)) == 2) {
        p1 <- wilcox.test(AneuploidyScore ~ CNV, data = subset)$p.value
        p2 <- summary(coxph(Surv(OS_MONTHS, OS) ~ AneuploidyScore, data = subset))$coefficients[,"Pr(>|z|)"]

        if (p1 < 0.05 && p2 < 0.05) {
          confounded_tumors <- c(confounded_tumors, tumor)
          break
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

unique(confounded_tumors)
```

arm-level 
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(survival)

# 1. Get all Arm columns
arm_cols <- grep("^Arm", names(df), value = TRUE)

# 2. Store output
confounded_results <- data.frame()

# 3. Loop through tumor Ă— arm Ă— Gain/Loss
for (tumor in unique(df$CANCER_TYPE)) {
  for (arm in arm_cols) {
    for (comparison in c("Gain", "Loss")) {

      subset <- df %>%
        filter(CANCER_TYPE == tumor,
               DRUG_TYPE == "PD-1/PDL-1",
               !!sym(arm) %in% c("None", comparison),
               !is.na(AneuploidyScore),
               !is.na(OS_MONTHS),
               !is.na(OS)) %>%
        mutate(CNV = factor(!!sym(arm), levels = c(comparison, "None")))

      if (nrow(subset) >= 10 && length(unique(subset$CNV)) == 2) {
        # 1. CNV ~ Aneuploidy
        p1 <- wilcox.test(AneuploidyScore ~ CNV, data = subset)$p.value

        # 2. Aneuploidy ~ Survival
        p2 <- summary(coxph(Surv(OS_MONTHS, OS) ~ AneuploidyScore, data = subset))$coefficients[,"Pr(>|z|)"]

        # If both significant, record as confounded
        if (p1 < 0.05 && p2 < 0.05) {
          confounded_results <- rbind(confounded_results, data.frame(
            Tumor = tumor,
            Arm = arm,
            Comparison = comparison,
            P_CNV_vs_Aneuploidy = signif(p1, 3),
            P_Aneuploidy_vs_Surv = signif(p2, 3)
          ))
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

# 4. Just show it â€” no saving, no files, no nonsense
confounded_results
```


test tumor purity 
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(survival)

# 1. Get all Arm columns
arm_cols <- grep("^Arm", names(df_cyto), value = TRUE)

# 2. Store output
purity_confounded_results <- data.frame()

# 3. Loop through tumor Ă— arm Ă— Gain/Loss
for (tumor in unique(df_cyto$CANCER_TYPE)) {
  for (arm in arm_cols) {
    for (comparison in c("Gain", "Loss")) {

      subset <- df_cyto %>%
        filter(CANCER_TYPE == tumor,
               DRUG_TYPE == "PD-1/PDL-1",
               !!sym(arm) %in% c("None", comparison),
               !is.na(TUMOR_PURITY),
               !is.na(OS_MONTHS),
               !is.na(OS)) %>%
        mutate(CNV = factor(!!sym(arm), levels = c(comparison, "None")))

      if (nrow(subset) >= 10 && length(unique(subset$CNV)) == 2) {
        # 1. CNV status associated with purity?
        p1 <- wilcox.test(TUMOR_PURITY ~ CNV, data = subset)$p.value

        # 2. Purity associated with survival?
        p2 <- summary(coxph(Surv(OS_MONTHS, OS) ~ TUMOR_PURITY, data = subset))$coefficients[, "Pr(>|z|)"]

        if (p1 < 0.05 && p2 < 0.05) {
          purity_confounded_results <- rbind(purity_confounded_results, data.frame(
            Tumor = tumor,
            Arm = arm,
            Comparison = comparison,
            P_CNV_vs_Purity = signif(p1, 3),
            P_Purity_vs_Surv = signif(p2, 3)
          ))
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

# 4. View the results
purity_confounded_results
```

## calcualte the covariate 
```{r}
# aneuploidy score 
# Identify cytoband-level columns
cytoband_cols <- grep("^[0-9XY]+[pq]", names(cnv_raw), value = TRUE)

# Compute absolute CNV value sum across all cytobands (per sample)
aneuploidy_cytoband <- cnv_raw %>%
  select(SAMPLE_ID, all_of(cytoband_cols)) %>%
  mutate(AneuploidyScore = rowSums(abs(across(all_of(cytoband_cols))), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  select(SAMPLE_ID, AneuploidyScore)
```

```{r}
# Summary of cleaned purity values
summary(clin_smp$TUMOR_PURITY)

# Count how many are not missing
sum(!is.na(clin_smp$TUMOR_PURITY))
```

## merge 

```{r}
# Merge tumor purity and aneuploidy score with sample metadata
sample_info_extended <- clin_smp %>%
  select(SAMPLE_ID, PATIENT_ID, CANCER_TYPE, ONCOTREE_CODE, TUMOR_PURITY) %>%
  left_join(aneuploidy_cytoband, by = "SAMPLE_ID")

# Merge with categorized CNV cytobands
merged_sample_cytoband <- merge(sample_info_extended, cnv_cat_cytoband, by = "SAMPLE_ID")

# Merge with patient-level survival info
df_cyto <- merge(
  clin_pt[, c("PATIENT_ID", "OS_MONTHS", "OS_STATUS")],
  merged_sample_cytoband,
  by = "PATIENT_ID"
) %>%
  mutate(
    OS = if_else(str_detect(OS_STATUS, "LIVING"), 0, 1)
  )
```


```{r}
# check if df_cyto contains all cytobands
colnames(df_cyto)[grep("^[0-9XY]+[pq]", colnames(df_cyto))]
```

## update Cox model to include both covariates 

### cancer type 
```{r}
results_cytoband <- data.frame()

for (tumor in cancer_types_to_plot) {
  tumor_data <- df_cyto %>% filter(CANCER_TYPE == tumor)

  for (band in cytoband_cols) {
    for (comparison in c("Gain", "Loss")) {
      subset <- tumor_data %>%
        filter(!!sym(band) %in% c("None", comparison)) %>%
        mutate(CNV = factor(!!sym(band), levels = c(comparison, "None"))) %>%
        filter(!is.na(TUMOR_PURITY), !is.na(AneuploidyScore))

      if (nrow(subset) >= 10 && length(unique(subset$CNV)) == 2) {
        tryCatch({
          fit <- coxph(Surv(OS_MONTHS, OS) ~ CNV + TUMOR_PURITY + AneuploidyScore, data = subset) # include the 2 covariates 

          # Dynamically find CNV coefficient (either CNVGain or CNVLoss)
          cnv_term <- grep("^CNV", names(coef(fit)), value = TRUE)
          if (length(cnv_term) != 1) next  # skip if CNV coefficient not found

          hr <- exp(coef(fit)[cnv_term])
          ci <- round(exp(confint(fit)[cnv_term, ]), 3)
          p <- summary(fit)$coefficients[cnv_term, 5]

          results_cytoband <- rbind(results_cytoband, data.frame(
            CANCER_TYPE = tumor,
            Cytoband = band,
            Comparison = comparison,
            HR = round(hr, 3),
            log2HR = log2(hr),
            CI = paste(ci[1], ci[2], sep = " - "),
            P = signif(p, 3),
            N = nrow(subset)
          ))
        }, error = function(e) {
          message(paste("Skipped:", tumor, band, comparison, "-", e$message))
        })
      }
    }
  }
}
```

check 
```{r}
dim(results_cytoband)
head(results_cytoband)
summary(results_cytoband$log2HR)
```

## Plot
```{r}
# Label cytobands by arm (to avoid crowding)
results_cytoband <- results_cytoband %>%
  mutate(
    Event = ifelse(Comparison == "Gain", "Gain", "Loss"),
    Chrom = str_extract(Cytoband, "^[0-9XY]+"),
    ArmLabel = str_extract(Cytoband, "^[0-9XY]+[pq]")
  )

# Order cytobands by chromosome
cyto_levels <- results_cytoband %>%
  mutate(
    chr_num = as.numeric(str_extract(Cytoband, "^[0-9]+")),
    chr_num = ifelse(is.na(chr_num), ifelse(str_detect(Cytoband, "^X"), 23, 24), chr_num),
    arm_suffix = str_extract(Cytoband, "[pq]$")
  ) %>%
  arrange(chr_num, arm_suffix) %>%
  pull(Cytoband) %>%
  unique()

results_cytoband$Cytoband <- factor(results_cytoband$Cytoband, levels = cyto_levels)

# Choose a label for each arm to avoid overcrowding
axis_labels <- results_cytoband %>%
  group_by(ArmLabel) %>%
  summarise(Cytoband = Cytoband[ceiling(n() / 2)], .groups = "drop")

# Create output folder
dir.create("logHR_plots_adjusted", showWarnings = FALSE)

# Loop over each cancer type and generate plots
for (tumor in unique(results_cytoband$CANCER_TYPE)) {
  plot_data <- results_cytoband %>% filter(CANCER_TYPE == tumor)

  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Cytoband, y = log2HR, fill = Event)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Gain" = "#D64045", "Loss" = "#375E97")) +
    scale_x_discrete(
      breaks = axis_labels$Cytoband,
      labels = axis_labels$ArmLabel
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Adjusted CNV Survival Effects in ", tumor),
      x = "Chromosome",
      y = "log2(Hazard Ratio)",
      fill = "CNV Event"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, size = 6),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
    )

  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("logHR_plots_adjusted/", gsub(" ", "_", tumor), "_logHR_adjusted.pdf"),
    plot = p, width = 10, height = 4
  )
}
```



### oncotree code
```{r}
# define oncotree IDs
oncotree_codes_to_plot <- c("LUAD", "SKCM", "CCRCC", "BLCA", "GBM", "COAD", "LUSC",
                            "MUP", "UTUC", "IDC", "STAD", "OPHSC", "ESCA", "UM", "HNSC")

# run adjusted Cox models again, this time stratify by oncotree code 
results_cytoband <- data.frame()

for (code in oncotree_codes_to_plot) {
  tumor_data <- df_cyto %>% filter(ONCOTREE_CODE == code)

  for (band in cytoband_cols) {
    for (comparison in c("Gain", "Loss")) {
      subset <- tumor_data %>%
        filter(!!sym(band) %in% c("None", comparison)) %>%
        mutate(CNV = factor(!!sym(band), levels = c(comparison, "None"))) %>%
        filter(!is.na(TUMOR_PURITY), !is.na(AneuploidyScore))

      if (nrow(subset) >= 10 && length(unique(subset$CNV)) == 2) {
        tryCatch({
          fit <- coxph(Surv(OS_MONTHS, OS) ~ CNV + TUMOR_PURITY + AneuploidyScore, data = subset)
          cnv_term <- grep("^CNV", names(coef(fit)), value = TRUE)
          if (length(cnv_term) != 1) next

          hr <- exp(coef(fit)[cnv_term])
          ci <- round(exp(confint(fit)[cnv_term, ]), 3)
          p <- summary(fit)$coefficients[cnv_term, 5]

          results_cytoband <- rbind(results_cytoband, data.frame(
            ONCOTREE_CODE = code,
            Cytoband = band,
            Comparison = comparison,
            HR = round(hr, 3),
            log2HR = log2(hr),
            CI = paste(ci[1], ci[2], sep = " - "),
            P = signif(p, 3),
            N = nrow(subset)
          ))
        }, error = function(e) {
          message(paste("Skipped:", code, band, comparison, "-", e$message))
        })
      }
    }
  }
}
```

```{r}
## Plot by oncotree code 
# Format for plotting
results_cytoband <- results_cytoband %>%
  mutate(
    Event = ifelse(Comparison == "Gain", "Gain", "Loss"),
    Chrom = str_extract(Cytoband, "^[0-9XY]+"),
    ArmLabel = str_extract(Cytoband, "^[0-9XY]+[pq]")
  )

# Order cytobands for x-axis
cyto_levels <- results_cytoband %>%
  mutate(
    chr_num = as.numeric(str_extract(Cytoband, "^[0-9]+")),
    chr_num = ifelse(is.na(chr_num), ifelse(str_detect(Cytoband, "^X"), 23, 24), chr_num),
    arm_suffix = str_extract(Cytoband, "[pq]$")
  ) %>%
  arrange(chr_num, arm_suffix) %>%
  pull(Cytoband) %>%
  unique()

results_cytoband$Cytoband <- factor(results_cytoband$Cytoband, levels = cyto_levels)

# Label 1 cytoband per arm
axis_labels <- results_cytoband %>%
  group_by(ArmLabel) %>%
  summarise(Cytoband = Cytoband[ceiling(n()/2)], .groups = "drop")

# Save individual plots
dir.create("logHR_plots_adjusted_oncotree", showWarnings = FALSE)

for (tumor in unique(results_cytoband$ONCOTREE_CODE)) {
  plot_data <- results_cytoband %>% filter(ONCOTREE_CODE == tumor)

  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Cytoband, y = log2HR, fill = Event)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Gain" = "#D64045", "Loss" = "#375E97")) +
    scale_x_discrete(
      breaks = axis_labels$Cytoband,
      labels = axis_labels$ArmLabel
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Adjusted CNV Survival Effects in ", tumor),
      x = "Chromosome",
      y = "log2(Hazard Ratio)",
      fill = "CNV Event"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, size = 6),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
    )

  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("logHR_plots_adjusted_oncotree/", tumor, "_logHR_adjusted.pdf"),
    plot = p, width = 10, height = 4
  )
}
```

```{r}
# compare adjusted vs undajsuted
results_cytoband$Model <- "Adjusted"
results_cytoband_unadj$Model <- "Unadjusted"

combined <- bind_rows(results_cytoband_unadj, results_cytoband)

ggplot(combined, aes(x = Cytoband, y = log2HR, fill = Model)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~ CANCER_TYPE, scales = "free_x") +
  theme_bw()
```


### filter for p value and account for aneuploidy score only if it confounds 
```{r}
for (tumor in unique(results_cytoband$CANCER_TYPE)) {
  plot_data <- results_cytoband %>%
    filter(CANCER_TYPE == tumor, P < 0.05)  # filter for p value 

  if (nrow(plot_data) == 0) next  # skip if no significant results

  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Cytoband, y = log2HR, fill = Event)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Gain" = "#D64045", "Loss" = "#375E97")) +
    scale_x_discrete(
      breaks = axis_labels$Cytoband,
      labels = axis_labels$ArmLabel
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Significant CNV Survival Effects in ", tumor),
      x = "Chromosome",
      y = "log2(Hazard Ratio)",
      fill = "CNV Event"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, size = 6),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
    )

  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("logHR_plots_filtered/", gsub(" ", "_", tumor), ".pdf"),
    plot = p, width = 10, height = 4
  )
}
```
